title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement [INSERT CONTACT METHOD]. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.0, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/0/ code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https:// www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Contributing to err
text: outlines propose change err.

title: Contributing to err
text: can fix typos, spelling mistakes, grammatical errors documentation directly using GitHub web interface, long changes made source file. generally means ’ll need edit roxygen2 comments .R, .Rd file. can find .R file generates .Rd reading comment first line.

title: Contributing to err
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed).

title: Contributing to err
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("", fork = TRUE). Install development dependences devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. Begin commit merge messages - indicate user facing changes included NEWS.md. use fledge automatically generate NEWS.md file.

title: Contributing to err
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use styler package apply styles, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Contributing to err
text: Please note err project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project agree abide terms.

title: Getting help with err
text: Thanks using err! filing issue, places explore pieces put together make process smooth possible.

title: Getting help with err
text: opening new issue, sure search issues pull requests make sure bug hasn’t reported /already fixed development version. default, search pre-populated :issue :open. can edit qualifiers (e.g. :pr, :closed) needed. example, ’d simply remove :open search issues repo, open closed.

title: Getting help with err
text: Start making minimal reproducible example using reprex package. haven’t heard used reprex , ’re treat! Seriously, reprex make R-question-asking endeavors easier (pretty insane ROI five ten minutes ’ll take learn ’s ). additional reprex pointers, check Get help! section tidyverse site.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Thorley J, Dunham J (2026). err: Customizable Object Sensitive Messages. R package version 0.2.0.9000, https://github.com/poissonconsulting/err.
code: @Manual{,   title = {err: Customizable Object Sensitive Messages},   author = {Joe Thorley and James Dunham},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/poissonconsulting/err}, }

title: Customizable Object Sensitive Messages
text: err human - Alexander Pope (1711) err light-weight R package produce customizable number object sensitive error warning messages.

title: Customizable Object Sensitive Messages
text: co functions produce object sensitive strings.
code: library(err)  fox <- c("The", "quick", "brown", "fox", "jumps", "over", "the", "lazy", "dog") co(fox) #> [1] "fox has 9 values: 'The', 'quick', 'brown', 'fox', 'jumps', 'over', 'the', 'lazy', 'dog'" co(fox[1]) #> [1] "fox[1] has 1 value: 'The'" co(fox[0]) #> [1] "fox[0] has 0 values" co(fox, nlots = 5) #> [1] "fox has 9 values: 'The', 'quick', 'brown', ..., 'dog'"

title: Customizable Object Sensitive Messages
text: object sensitive strings fully customized. following sprintf-like types can used custom messages: %c: object comma separated list (produced cc function) %n: length object %o: name object %s: ‘s’ n != 1 otherwise ’’ %r: ‘’ n != 1 otherwise ‘’ various formatting options
code: one <- "darn! the vector %o of length %n has the following value: %c" none <- "phew! vector %o is empty" some <- "rats! vector %o has the following %n element%s: %c" lots <- "really?! the %n elements of vector %o are too numerous to print"  co(fox[0], one = one, none = none, some = some, lots = lots, nlots = 5) #> [1] "phew! vector fox[0] is empty" co(fox[1], one = one, none = none, some = some, lots = lots, nlots = 5) #> [1] "darn! the vector fox[1] of length 1 has the following value: 'The'" co(fox[1:3], one = one, none = none, some = some, lots = lots, nlots = 5) #> [1] "rats! vector fox[1:3] has the following 3 elements: 'The', 'quick', 'brown'" co(fox[1:5], one = one, none = none, some = some, lots = lots, nlots = 5) #> [1] "really?! the 5 elements of vector fox[1:5] are too numerous to print" co(fox[1:6], conjunction = "or", bracket = "|", oxford = TRUE, ellipsis = 5) #> [1] "fox[1:6] has 6 values: |The|, |quick|, |brown|, ..., or |over|"

title: Customizable Object Sensitive Messages
text: also method data frames.
code: cat(co(datasets::mtcars, conjunction = "and", oxford = TRUE, ellipsis = 5)) #> datasets::mtcars has 11 columns #> mpg: 21, 21, 22.8, ..., and 21.4 #> cyl: 6, 6, 4, ..., and 4 #> disp: 160, 160, 108, ..., and 121 #> ... #> and carb: 4, 4, 1, ..., and 2

title: Customizable Object Sensitive Messages
text: cn function produces number sensitive customizable messages
code: cn(0) #> [1] "there are 0 values" cn(1) #> [1] "there is 1 value" cn(2) #> [1] "there are 2 values" cn(100, lots = "there %r %n value%s - this is a lot") #> [1] "there are 100 values - this is a lot"

title: Customizable Object Sensitive Messages
text: co cn functions can combined wrappers msg, wrn err produce message, warning error (without call part warning/error message).
code: msg(cn(2)) #> there are 2 values wrn(cn(2)) #> Warning: there are 2 values err(cn(2)) #> Error: there are 2 values

title: Customizable Object Sensitive Messages
text: install latest release version CRAN install latest development version GitHub
code: install.packages("err") if(!"devtools" %in% installed.packages()[,1])    install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("poissonconsulting/err")

title: Concatenation with Commas — cc
text: Concatenates object values string value separated comma possibly last value separated conjunction.

title: Concatenation with Commas — cc
code: # Default S3 method cc(   object,   conjunction = NULL,   bracket = "",   ellipsis = 10,   oxford = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'character' cc(   object,   conjunction = NULL,   bracket = "'",   ellipsis = 10,   oxford = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'factor' cc(   object,   conjunction = NULL,   bracket = "'",   ellipsis = 10,   oxford = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'data.frame' cc(object, conjunction = NULL, ellipsis = 10, oxford = FALSE, ...)

title: Concatenation with Commas — cc
text: object object values concatenate. conjunction string conjunction separate last value NULL. bracket string bracket values . ellipsis count total number values required use ellipsis. oxford flag indicating whether use Oxford comma (conjunction). ... Unused

title: Customizable Number Aware String — cn
text: Customizable Number Aware String

title: Customizable Number Aware String — cn
code: cn(   n,   one = "there %r %n value%s",   some = one,   none = some,   lots = some,   nlots = 10 )

title: Customizable Number Aware String — cn
text: n count number. one string return n = 1 string return n 2, 3, ..., nlots - 1 none string return n = 0 lots string return n >= nlots nlots count number values consider lots

title: Customizable Number Aware String — cn
text: string updated message.

title: Customizable Number Aware String — cn
text: following sprintf-like types can used custom messages: n length object s 's' n != 1 otherwise ”

title: Customizable Number Aware String — cn
code: cn(0) #> [1] "there are 0 values" cn(1) #> [1] "there is 1 value" cn(4) #> [1] "there are 4 values"

title: Customizable Object Aware String — co
text: Produces fully customizable object aware string consecutive values separated columns.

title: Customizable Object Aware String — co
code: # Default S3 method co(   object,   one = "%o has %n value%s: %c",   some = one,   none = gsub(": ", "", some),   lots = some,   nlots = 10,   conjunction = NULL,   bracket = "",   ellipsis = nlots,   oxford = FALSE,   object_name = substitute(object),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'character' co(   object,   one = "%o has %n value%s: %c",   some = one,   none = gsub(": ", "", some),   lots = some,   nlots = 10,   conjunction = NULL,   bracket = "'",   ellipsis = nlots,   oxford = FALSE,   object_name = substitute(object),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'factor' co(   object,   one = "%o has %n value%s: %c",   some = one,   none = gsub(": ", "", some),   lots = some,   nlots = 10,   conjunction = NULL,   bracket = "'",   ellipsis = nlots,   oxford = FALSE,   object_name = substitute(object),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'data.frame' co(   object,   one = "%o has %n column%s\n%c",   some = one,   none = none,   lots = some,   nlots = 10,   conjunction = NULL,   ellipsis = nlots,   oxford = FALSE,   object_name = substitute(object),   ... )

title: Customizable Object Aware String — co
text: object object length n one string return n = 1 string return n 2, 3, ..., nlots - 1 none string return n = 0 lots string return n >= nlots nlots count number values consider lots conjunction string conjunction separate last value NULL. bracket string bracket values . ellipsis count total number values required use ellipsis. oxford flag indicating whether use Oxford comma (conjunction). object_name string object name. ... Unused.

title: Customizable Object Aware String — co
text: following sprintf-like types can used custom messages: c object comma separated list (produced cc function) n length object o name object s 's' n != 1 otherwise ” r '' n != 1 otherwise ''
